title: MCP tool-handler layer has no unit tests
evidence: BrazilianSoccerMcpServer.java toolCall handlers contain arg-parsing, filtering and formatting logic; no test under src/test references the server class (tests only cover loader/service layer)
suggestion: Extract handler logic into testable units or add tests exercising the 6 tools, especially given the TDD prompt

title: Blank/NaN goals and seasons parse to 0 instead of being treated as missing
evidence: MatchLoader.java:30 parseGoals and parseSeason return 0 on blank/NaN, conflating missing scores with a genuine 0-0 result and skewing standings/average-goals aggregates
suggestion: Skip or flag rows with missing scores rather than coercing to 0

title: Match filtering supports season only, not calendar date range
evidence: MatchService.java has findBySeason but no date-range filter; Match.datetime is stored as a raw unparsed string. R4 is satisfied via season (the spec asks date-range and/or season)
suggestion: Parse datetime and add from/to date filtering to fully cover the date-range option

title: Several public service methods are unused by the server
evidence: StatisticsService.getHomeRecord, PlayerService.getTopPlayersByClub, MatchService.findByTeams and findBySeasonAndCompetition are not called from any tool handler
suggestion: Wire these into tools or remove to reduce surface area

title: Competition filter requires an exact (equalsIgnoreCase) name
evidence: BrazilianSoccerMcpServer find_matches / get_biggest_wins filter via m.competition().equalsIgnoreCase(comp); a query like 'Brasileirão' or partial names will not match the stored 'Brasileirao'
suggestion: Normalize/contains-match competition names like the team normalizer does

title: Aggregate stats beyond the spec minimum
evidence: StatisticsService implements getBiggestWins and getAverageGoalsPerMatch, and find_players supports top-N ranking — exceeds R10's single-aggregate requirement
